title: Iberian Crossroads
subtitle: Translation, transmission, and the pseudo-Lullian corpus

narrative.intro: Toledo and the translation movement made Iberia the door through which Arabic alchemy entered Latin Europe; later the peninsula grew its own pseudo-Lullian tradition.
narrative.provenance_note: Regions = 'Iberia' (40 events) + 'Catalonia' (1). Events from the ALCHEMYTIMELINEMAP corpus (largely draft-status); locations are event geocodes, not residences. Absence on this map reflects gaps in the record.
narrative.design_note: Modern Atlas: the translation crossroads needs maximum legibility — routes and centres read plainly, nothing ornamental in the way.
